player_notes.luka doncic: Doncic is sidelined (did not play Game 1 vs. Rockets). Monitor availability for Game 2; his absence significantly elevates LeBron's usage and assists ceiling.
player_notes.austin reaves: Reaves did not play Game 1 vs. Rockets due to injury. Monitor status for Game 2; absence boosts Kennard and LeBron roles.
player_notes.kevin durant: Durant missed Game 1 for Houston due to injury. Monitor status for Game 2; his absence hurt Rockets' offense and may continue to affect Houston player props.
player_notes.lebron james: With Doncic and Reaves out, LeBron played a triple-threat role (19 pts, 13 ast, 8 reb in Game 1). Assists prop ceiling elevated while both remain sidelined.
player_notes.luke kennard: Scored a career playoff-high 27 points in Game 1 with Doncic and Reaves absent. Role and shot volume elevated while key Lakers are out.
player_notes.jamal murray: Went 16-for-16 from the free throw line in Game 1 vs. Timberwolves; high-volume, high-efficiency performance. Note Jokic and Gordon were quiet in the first half, suggesting Murray absorbed usage.
player_notes.jayson tatum: Returned from Achilles rupture for Game 1 playoff debut, posting 25 pts, 11 reb, 7 ast vs. 76ers. Cleared and playing full role; strong prop baseline confirmed.

game_notes.HOU vs LAL: Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves (LAL) and Kevin Durant (HOU) all missed Game 1. Monitor all three for Game 2 availability — their statuses drastically shift usage and prop values across both rosters.
